NKJV.1: We then, as workers together with Him also plead with you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
NKJV.2: For He says: “In an acceptable time I have heard you, And in the day of salvation I have helped you.” Behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.
NKJV.3: We give no offense in anything, that our ministry may not be blamed.
NKJV.4: But in all things we commend ourselves as ministers of God: in much patience, in tribulations, in needs, in distresses,
NKJV.5: in stripes, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labors, in sleeplessness, in fastings;
NKJV.6: by purity, by knowledge, by longsuffering, by kindness, by the Holy Spirit, by sincere love,
NKJV.7: by the word of truth, by the power of God, by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
NKJV.8: by honor and dishonor, by evil report and good report; as deceivers, and yet true;
NKJV.9: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold we live; as chastened, and yet not killed;
NKJV.10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
NKJV.11: O Corinthians! We have spoken openly to you, our heart is wide open.
NKJV.12: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.
NKJV.13: Now in return for the same (I speak as to children), you also be open.
NKJV.14: Do not be unequally yoked together with unbelievers. For what fellowship has righteousness with lawlessness? And what communion has light with darkness?
NKJV.15: And what accord has Christ with Belial? Or what part has a believer with an unbeliever?
NKJV.16: And what agreement has the temple of God with idols? For you are the temple of the living God. As God has said:  “I will dwell in them And walk among them. I will be their God, And they shall be My people.”
NKJV.17: Therefore  “Come out from among them And be separate, says the Lord. Do not touch what is unclean, And I will receive you.”
NKJV.18: “I will be a Father to you, And you shall be My sons and daughters, Says the LORD Almighty.”

ESV.1: Working together with him, then, we appeal to you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
ESV.2: For he says, “In a favorable time I listened to you, and in a day of salvation I have helped you.”  Behold, now is the favorable time; behold, now is the day of salvation.
ESV.3: We put no obstacle in anyone's way, so that no fault may be found with our ministry,
ESV.4: but as servants of God we commend ourselves in every way: by great endurance, in afflictions, hardships, calamities,
ESV.5: beatings, imprisonments, riots, labors, sleepless nights, hunger;
ESV.6: by purity, knowledge, patience, kindness, the Holy Spirit, genuine love;
ESV.7: by truthful speech, and the power of God; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left;
ESV.8: through honor and dishonor, through slander and praise. We are treated as impostors, and yet are true;
ESV.9: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and yet not killed;
ESV.10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, yet possessing everything.
ESV.11: We have spoken freely to you,  Corinthians; our heart is wide open.
ESV.12: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted in your own affections.
ESV.13: In return (I speak as to children) widen your hearts also.
ESV.14: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership has righteousness with lawlessness? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?
ESV.15: What accord has Christ with Belial?  Or what portion does a believer share with an unbeliever?
ESV.16: What agreement has the temple of God with idols? For we are the temple of the living God; as God said, “I will make my dwelling among them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
ESV.17: Therefore go out from their midst, and be separate from them, says the Lord, and touch no unclean thing; then I will welcome you,
ESV.18: and I will be a father to you, and you shall be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord Almighty.”

NASB.1: And working together [with Him], we also urge you not to receive the grace of God in vain--
NASB.2: for He says, 'AT THE ACCEPTABLE TIME I LISTENED TO YOU, AND ON THE DAY OF SALVATION I HELPED YOU.' Behold, now is 'THE ACCEPTABLE TIME,' behold, now is 'THE DAY OF SALVATION '--
NASB.3: giving no cause for offense in anything, so that the ministry will not be discredited,
NASB.4: but in everything commending ourselves as servants of God, in much endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,
NASB.5: in beatings, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labors, in sleeplessness, in hunger,
NASB.6: in purity, in knowledge, in patience, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in genuine love,
NASB.7: in the word of truth, in the power of God; by the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and the left,
NASB.8: by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report; [regarded] as deceivers and yet true;
NASB.9: as unknown yet well-known, as dying yet behold, we live; as punished yet not put to death,
NASB.10: as sorrowful yet always rejoicing, as poor yet making many rich, as having nothing yet possessing all things.
NASB.11: Our mouth has spoken freely to you, O Corinthians, our heart is opened wide.
NASB.12: You are not restrained by us, but you are restrained in your own affections.
NASB.13: Now in a like exchange-- I speak as to children-- open wide [to us] also.
NASB.14: Do not be bound together with unbelievers; for what partnership have righteousness and lawlessness, or what fellowship has light with darkness?
NASB.15: Or what harmony has Christ with Belial, or what has a believer in common with an unbeliever?
NASB.16: Or what agreement has the temple of God with idols? For we are the temple of the living God; just as God said, 'I WILL DWELL IN THEM AND WALK AMONG THEM; AND I WILL BE THEIR GOD, AND THEY SHALL BE MY PEOPLE.
NASB.17: 'Therefore, COME OUT FROM THEIR MIDST AND BE SEPARATE,' says the Lord. 'AND DO NOT TOUCH WHAT IS UNCLEAN; And I will welcome you.
NASB.18: 'And I will be a father to you, And you shall be sons and daughters to Me,' Says the Lord Almighty.

NLT.1: As God’s partners, we beg you not to accept this marvelous gift of God’s kindness and then ignore it.
NLT.2:
For God says, “At just the right time, I heard you.
On the day of salvation, I helped you.”
Indeed, the “right time” is now. Today is the day of salvation.
NLT.3: We live in such a way that no one will stumble because of us, and no one will find fault with our ministry.
NLT.4: In everything we do, we show that we are true ministers of God. We patiently endure troubles and hardships and calamities of every kind.
NLT.5: We have been beaten, been put in prison, faced angry mobs, worked to exhaustion, endured sleepless nights, and gone without food.
NLT.6: We prove ourselves by our purity, our understanding, our patience, our kindness, by the Holy Spirit within us, and by our sincere love.
NLT.7: We faithfully preach the truth. God’s power is working in us. We use the weapons of righteousness in the right hand for attack and the left hand for defense.
NLT.8: We serve God whether people honor us or despise us, whether they slander us or praise us. We are honest, but they call us impostors.
NLT.9: We are ignored, even though we are well known. We live close to death, but we are still alive. We have been beaten, but we have not been killed.
NLT.10: Our hearts ache, but we always have joy. We are poor, but we give spiritual riches to others. We own nothing, and yet we have everything.
NLT.11: Oh, dear Corinthian friends! We have spoken honestly with you, and our hearts are open to you.
NLT.12: There is no lack of love on our part, but you have withheld your love from us.
NLT.13: I am asking you to respond as if you were my own children. Open your hearts to us!
NLT.14: Don’t team up with those who are unbelievers. How can righteousness be a partner with wickedness? How can light live with darkness?
NLT.15: What harmony can there be between Christ and the devil ? How can a believer be a partner with an unbeliever?
NLT.16:
And what union can there be between God’s temple and idols? For we are the temple of the living God. As God said: “I will live in them
and walk among them.
I will be their God,
and they will be my people.
NLT.17:
Therefore, come out from among unbelievers,
and separate yourselves from them, says the LORD.
Don’t touch their filthy things,
and I will welcome you.
NLT.18:
And I will be your Father,
and you will be my sons and daughters,
says the LORD Almighty. ”

WEB.1: Working together, we entreat also that you not receive the grace of God in vain,
WEB.2: for he says, “At an acceptable time I listened to you, in a day of salvation I helped you.”   Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation.
WEB.3: We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed,
WEB.4: but in everything commending ourselves, as servants of God, in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,
WEB.5: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings;
WEB.6: in pureness, in knowledge, in perseverance, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in sincere love,
WEB.7: in the word of truth, in the power of God; by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
WEB.8: by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report; as deceivers, and yet true;
WEB.9: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and not killed;
WEB.10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
WEB.11: Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged.
WEB.12: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.
WEB.13: Now in return, I speak as to my children, you also be open wide.
WEB.14: Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers, for what fellowship have righteousness and iniquity? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?
WEB.15: What agreement has Christ with Belial? Or what portion has a believer with an unbeliever?
WEB.16: What agreement has a temple of God with idols? For you are a temple of the living God. Even as God said, “I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
WEB.17: Therefore “‘Come out from among them, and be separate,’ says the Lord. ‘Touch no unclean thing. I will receive you.
WEB.18: I will be to you a Father. You will be to me sons and daughters,’  says the Lord Almighty.”

CSB17.1: Working together with him, we also appeal to you, “Don’t receive the grace of God in vain.”
CSB17.2:
For he says: At an acceptable time I listened to you,
and in the day of salvation I helped you. [1] ⓔ
See, now is the acceptable time; now is the day of salvation! ⓕ
CSB17.3: We are not giving anyone an occasion for offense, so that the ministry will not be blamed.
CSB17.4: Instead, as God’s ministers, we commend ourselves in everything: by great endurance, by afflictions, by hardships, by difficulties,
CSB17.5: by beatings, by imprisonments, <sup>ⓖ</sup> by riots, by labors, by sleepless nights, by times of hunger,
CSB17.6: by purity, <sup>ⓗ</sup> by knowledge, <sup>ⓘ</sup> by patience, <sup>ⓙ</sup> by kindness, <sup>ⓚ</sup> by the Holy Spirit, by sincere love, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: by the word of truth, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> by the power of God; <sup>ⓝ</sup> through weapons of righteousness for the right hand and the left,
CSB17.8: through glory <sup>ⓐ</sup> and dishonor, through slander and good report; regarded as deceivers, yet true;
CSB17.9: as unknown, yet recognized; as dying, yet see — we live; as being disciplined, yet not killed;
CSB17.10: as grieving, yet always rejoicing; <sup>ⓑ</sup> as poor, yet enriching many; as having nothing, yet possessing everything.
CSB17.11: We have spoken openly to you, Corinthians; our heart has been opened wide.
CSB17.12: We are not withholding our affection from you, but you are withholding yours from us.
CSB17.13: I speak as to my children; as a proper response, open your heart to us.
CSB17.14: Don’t become partners with those who do not believe. For what partnership is there between righteousness and lawlessness? <sup>ⓒ</sup> Or what fellowship does light <sup>ⓓ</sup> have with darkness? <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.15: What agreement does Christ have with Belial? <sup>[3]</sup> Or what does a believer have in common with an unbeliever?
CSB17.16:
And what agreement does the temple of God have with idols? ⓕ For we [4] are the temple of the living God, as God said: I will dwell ⓖ
and walk among them,
and I will be their God, ⓗ
and they will be my people. [5] ⓘ
CSB17.17:
Therefore, come out from among them
and be separate, says the Lord;
do not touch any unclean thing,
and I will welcome you. [6] ⓙ
CSB17.18:
And I will be a Father ⓚ to you,
and you will be sons ⓛ and daughters to me,
says the Lord Almighty. [7] ⓜ

AMP.1: Working together with Him, we strongly urge you not to receive God's grace in vain [by turning away from sound doctrine and His merciful kindness].
AMP.2:
For He says,
"At the acceptable time (the time of grace) I listened to you,
And I helped you on the day of salvation."
Behold, now is "the acceptable time," behold, now is "the day of salvation"—
AMP.3: we put no obstruction in anyone's path, so that the ministry will not be discredited,
AMP.4: but we commend ourselves in every way as servants of God: in great endurance, in sufferings, in hardships, in distresses,
AMP.5: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in sleepless nights, in hunger,
AMP.6: in purity and sincerity, in knowledge and spiritual insight, in patience, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in genuine love,
AMP.7: in [speaking] the word of truth, in the power of God; by the weapons of righteousness for the right hand [like holding the sword to attack] and for the left [like holding the shield to defend],
AMP.8: amid glory and dishonor; by evil report and good report; branded as deceivers and yet [vindicated as] truthful;
AMP.9: as unknown [to the world], yet well-known [by God and His people]; as dying, yet we live; as punished, yet not killed;
AMP.10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet bestowing riches on many; as having nothing, yet possessing all things.
AMP.11: We are speaking freely to you, Corinthians [we are keeping nothing back], and our heart is opened wide.
AMP.12: There is no limit to our affection for you, but you are limited in your own affection [for us].
AMP.13: Now in the same way as a fair exchange [for our love toward you]—I am speaking as [I would] to children—open wide [your hearts] to us also.
AMP.14: Do not be unequally bound together with unbelievers [do not make mismatched alliances with them, inconsistent with your faith]. For what partnership can righteousness have with lawlessness? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?
AMP.15: What harmony can there be between Christ and Belial (Satan)? Or what does a believer have in common with an unbeliever?
AMP.16:
What agreement is there between the temple of God and idols? For we are the temple of the living God; just as God said:
"I will dwell among them and walk among them;
And I will be their God, and they shall be My people.
AMP.17:
"So come out from among unbelievers and be separate," says the Lord,
"And do not touch what is unclean;
And I will graciously receive you and welcome you [with favor],
AMP.18:
And I will be a Father to you,
And you will be My sons and daughters,"
Says the Lord Almighty.

MSG.1: Companions as we are in this work with you, we beg you, please don't squander one bit of this marvelous life God has given us.
MSG.2: God reminds us, I heard your call in the nick of time; The day you needed me, I was there to help. Well, now is the right time to listen, the day to be helped.
MSG.3: Don't put it off; don't frustrate God's work by showing up late, throwing a question mark over everything we're doing.
MSG.4: Our work as God's servants gets validated—or not—in the details. People are watching us as we stay at our post, alertly, unswervingly . . . in hard times, tough times, bad times;
MSG.5: when we're beaten up, jailed, and mobbed; working hard, working late, working without eating;
MSG.6: with pure heart, clear head, steady hand; in gentleness, holiness, and honest love;
MSG.7: when we're telling the truth, and when God's showing his power; when we're doing our best setting things right;
MSG.8: when we're praised, and when we're blamed; slandered, and honored; true to our word, though distrusted;
MSG.9: ignored by the world, but recognized by God; terrifically alive, though rumored to be dead; beaten within an inch of our lives, but refusing to die;
MSG.10: immersed in tears, yet always filled with deep joy; living on handouts, yet enriching many; having nothing, having it all.
MSG.11: Dear, dear Corinthians, I can't tell you how much I long for you to enter this wide-open, spacious life.
MSG.12: We didn't fence you in. The smallness you feel comes from within you. Your lives aren't small, but you're living them in a small way.
MSG.13: I'm speaking as plainly as I can and with great affection. Open up your lives. Live openly and expansively!
MSG.14: Don't become partners with those who reject God. How can you make a partnership out of right and wrong? That's not partnership; that's war. Is light best friends with dark?
MSG.15: Does Christ go strolling with the Devil? Do trust and mistrust hold hands?
MSG.16: Who would think of setting up pagan idols in God's holy Temple? But that is exactly what we are, each of us a temple in whom God lives. God himself put it this way: "I'll live in them, move into them; I'll be their God and they'll be my people.
MSG.17: So leave the corruption and compromise; leave it for good," says God. "Don't link up with those who will pollute you. I want you all for myself.
MSG.18: I'll be a Father to you; you'll be sons and daughters to me." The Word of the Master, God.

NIV.1: As God’s fellow workers we urge you not to receive God’s grace in vain.
NIV.2:
For he says,
“In the time of my favor I heard you,
and in the day of salvation I helped you.”
I tell you, now is the time of God’s favor, now is the day of salvation.
NIV.3:
Paul’s Hardships
We put no stumbling block in anyone’s path, so that our ministry will not be discredited.
NIV.4: Rather, as servants of God we commend ourselves in every way: in great endurance; in troubles, hardships and distresses;
NIV.5: in beatings, imprisonments and riots; in hard work, sleepless nights and hunger;
NIV.6: in purity, understanding, patience and kindness; in the Holy Spirit and in sincere love;
NIV.7: in truthful speech and in the power of God; with weapons of righteousness in the right hand and in the left;
NIV.8: through glory and dishonor, bad report and good report; genuine, yet regarded as impostors;
NIV.9: known, yet regarded as unknown; dying, and yet we live on; beaten, and yet not killed;
NIV.10: sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; poor, yet making many rich; having nothing, and yet possessing everything.
NIV.11: We have spoken freely to you, Corinthians, and opened wide our hearts to you.
NIV.12: We are not withholding our affection from you, but you are withholding yours from us.
NIV.13: As a fair exchange—I speak as to my children—open wide your hearts also.
NIV.14:
Do Not Be Yoked With Unbelievers
Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?
NIV.15: What harmony is there between Christ and Belial? What does a believer have in common with an unbeliever?
NIV.16: What agreement is there between the temple of God and idols? For we are the temple of the living God. As God has said: “I will live with them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
NIV.17:
“Therefore come out from them
and be separate,
says the Lord.
Touch no unclean thing,
and I will receive you.”
NIV.18:
“I will be a Father to you,
and you will be my sons and daughters,
says the Lord Almighty.”

NRSVCE.1: As we work together with him, we urge you also not to accept the grace of God in vain.
NRSVCE.2:
For he says, “At an acceptable time I have listened to you,
and on a day of salvation I have helped you.”
See, now is the acceptable time; see, now is the day of salvation!
NRSVCE.3: We are putting no obstacle in anyone’s way, so that no fault may be found with our ministry,
NRSVCE.4: but as servants of God we have commended ourselves in every way: through great endurance, in afflictions, hardships, calamities,
NRSVCE.5: beatings, imprisonments, riots, labors, sleepless nights, hunger;
NRSVCE.6: by purity, knowledge, patience, kindness, holiness of spirit, genuine love,
NRSVCE.7: truthful speech, and the power of God; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left;
NRSVCE.8: in honor and dishonor, in ill repute and good repute. We are treated as impostors, and yet are true;
NRSVCE.9: as unknown, and yet are well known; as dying, and see — we are alive; as punished, and yet not killed;
NRSVCE.10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing everything.
NRSVCE.11: We have spoken frankly to you Corinthians; our heart is wide open to you.
NRSVCE.12: There is no restriction in our affections, but only in yours.
NRSVCE.13: In return — I speak as to children — open wide your hearts also.
NRSVCE.14: Do not be mismatched with unbelievers. For what partnership is there between righteousness and lawlessness? Or what fellowship is there between light and darkness?
NRSVCE.15: What agreement does Christ have with Beliar? Or what does a believer share with an unbeliever?
NRSVCE.16:
What agreement has the temple of God with idols? For we are the temple of the living God; as God said, “I will live in them and walk among them,
and I will be their God,
and they shall be my people.
NRSVCE.17:
Therefore come out from them,
and be separate from them, says the Lord,
and touch nothing unclean;
then I will welcome you,
NRSVCE.18:
and I will be your father,
and you shall be my sons and daughters,
says the Lord Almighty.”

NET.1: Now because we are fellow workers, we also urge you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
NET.2: For he says, “I heard you at the acceptable time, and in the day of salvation I helped you.” Look, now is the acceptable time; look, now is the day of salvation!
NET.3: We do not give anyone an occasion for taking an offense in anything, so that no fault may be found with our ministry.
NET.4: But as God’s servants, we have commended ourselves in every way, with great endurance, in persecutions, in difficulties, in distresses,
NET.5: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in troubles, in sleepless nights, in hunger,
NET.6: by purity, by knowledge, by patience, by benevolence, by the Holy Spirit, by genuine love,
NET.7: by truthful teaching, by the power of God, with weapons of righteousness both for the right hand and for the left,
NET.8: through glory and dishonor, through slander and praise; regarded as impostors, and yet true;
NET.9: as unknown, and yet well-known; as dying and yet — see! — we continue to live; as those who are scourged and yet not executed;
NET.10: as sorrowful, but always rejoicing, as poor, but making many rich, as having nothing, and yet possessing everything.
NET.11: We have spoken freely to you, Corinthians; our heart has been opened wide to you.
NET.12: Our affection for you is not restricted, but you are restricted in your affections for us.
NET.13: Now as a fair exchange — I speak as to my children — open wide your hearts to us also.
NET.14: Do not become partners with those who do not believe, for what partnership is there between righteousness and lawlessness, or what fellowship does light have with darkness?
NET.15: And what agreement does Christ have with Beliar? Or what does a believer share in common with an unbeliever?
NET.16: And what mutual agreement does the temple of God have with idols? For we are the temple of the living God, just as God said, “I will live in them and will walk among them, and I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
NET.17: Therefore “come out from their midst, and be separate,” says the Lord, “and touch no unclean thing, and I will welcome you,
NET.18: and I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters,” says the All-Powerful Lord.
